content.problemStatement:
A food fair is being organised in Chef's town. NNN booths were set up in a straight line and numbered 111 through NNN. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the probability with which the food served at booth iii will cause food poisoning at the beginning of the day is PiP_iPi​.

Over the course of the day, the Chef observes that certain booths might decide to offer less unhealthy food based on feedback from customers. You should process QQQ queries of the following two types:


0  L  R0\;L\;R0LR — Compute the probability that eating at each booth between booths LLL and RRR (inclusive) does not cause food poisoning.
1  L  R  T1\;L\;R\;T1LRT — Each booth between booths LLL and RRR (inclusive) starts offering food that is TTT times more likely to cause food poisoning.
constraints: All decimals on the input will be given with at most 6 fractional digits
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers N and Q. The second line contains N space-separated decimal numbers P1, P2, P3, …, PN. Each of the following Q lines describes one query in the format 0 L R or 1 L R T.
content.outputFormat: For each query of type 0, print a single line containing one real number — the probability of not getting food poisoning. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute error doesn't exceed 10^-6.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The answer to the first query is (1 - 0.1) * (1 - 0.25) * (1 - 0.85) = 0.10125
The answer to the first query is (1 - 0.5) * (1 - 0.5) = 0.25

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a simple iterative approach. We will first read the input and store the probabilities in an array. Then, for each query, we will calculate the probability of not getting food poisoning by multiplying the probabilities of not getting food poisoning at each booth. If the query is of type 1, we will update the probabilities of the booths accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q * N) — because for each query, we are potentially iterating over all booths.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — because we need to store the probabilities of all booths.
